description: Fix bugs and quality gaps found during Plugin SDK browser-context audit: HTTP status mapping errors in fetch utilities, missing AbortSignal support in public timing/DOM APIs, silent data loss in storage writes, and test coverage gaps.

userStories.title: Fix httpStatusToToolError to map 400 to validation and 408 to retryable timeout
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want HTTP 400 responses to produce a ToolError with category 'validation' and HTTP 408 responses to produce a retryable ToolError with category 'timeout', so that AI agents receive accurate error metadata for retry decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: httpStatusToToolError maps HTTP 400 to ToolError with category='validation', code='VALIDATION_ERROR', retryable=false
httpStatusToToolError maps HTTP 408 to ToolError with category='timeout', code='TIMEOUT', retryable=true
httpStatusToToolError maps HTTP 422 to ToolError with category='validation', code='VALIDATION_ERROR', retryable=false
All other 4xx status codes (405, 409, etc.) continue to use the existing catch-all behavior (category='internal', retryable=false)
Existing tests for 401, 403, 404, 429, 500, 503 still pass unchanged
New unit tests in fetch.test.ts cover 400, 408, and 422 status codes via the test HTTP server
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Add AbortSignal support to sleep()
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want sleep() to accept an optional AbortSignal so that long sleeps in tool handlers can be cancelled when the tool call is aborted.
acceptanceCriteria: sleep(ms, opts?) accepts an optional { signal?: AbortSignal } second parameter
When signal is already aborted, sleep rejects immediately with the signal's reason
When signal is aborted during the sleep, sleep rejects promptly with the signal's reason (does not wait for full timeout)
When no signal is provided, behavior is identical to the current implementation
The timer is cleared when the signal fires (no leaked timers)
The abort listener is removed when the timer fires (no leaked listeners)
New unit tests in timing.test.ts cover: already-aborted signal, signal aborted mid-sleep, no signal (backward compat), DOMException when aborted without custom reason
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Add AbortSignal support to waitForSelector and waitForSelectorRemoval
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want waitForSelector() and waitForSelectorRemoval() to accept an optional AbortSignal so that pending DOM waits can be cancelled when a tool call is aborted.
acceptanceCriteria: WaitForSelectorOptions includes an optional signal?: AbortSignal field
waitForSelector rejects immediately when signal is already aborted
waitForSelector rejects promptly when signal is aborted during the wait, disconnecting the MutationObserver and clearing the timeout timer
waitForSelectorRemoval rejects immediately when signal is already aborted
waitForSelectorRemoval rejects promptly when signal is aborted during the wait, disconnecting the MutationObserver and clearing the timeout timer
When no signal is provided, behavior is identical to the current implementation
Abort listeners are cleaned up on success, timeout, and abort (no leaked listeners)
New unit tests in dom.test.ts cover: already-aborted signal for both functions, signal aborted mid-wait for both functions, cleanup verification
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.ts, lines 11-14 (WaitForSelectorOptions), lines 30-87 (waitForSelector), lines 93-149 (waitForSelectorRemoval).

Add `signal?: AbortSignal` to the WaitForSelectorOptions interface.

In both waitForSelector and waitForSelectorRemoval, after checking for an existing element, add abort handling following the same pattern used in waitUntil (platform/plugin-sdk/src/timing.ts lines 96-152):

1. Check `signal?.aborted` early and reject immediately if so
2. Add an abort listener that sets `settled = true`, clears the timer, disconnects the observer, and rejects with the signal's reason
3. Clean up the abort listener in both the timer handler and the observer success path (add `signal?.removeEventListener('abort', onAbort)` alongside the existing `clearTimeout(timer)` and `observer.disconnect()` calls)

For the abort reason, use the same pattern as timing.ts: `signal.reason instanceof Error ? signal.reason : new Error('waitForSelector: aborted')`.

Tests: platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.test.ts. Note the tests use happy-dom. Create AbortController in the test, abort it, and verify the promise rejects. For mid-wait abort, start waitForSelector for a non-existent element with a long timeout, then abort after a short delay and verify prompt rejection.

userStories.title: Add log.warn on silent storage write failures
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want storage write failures (SecurityError, QuotaExceededError) to emit a log.warn() message so that I can diagnose why my plugin's data is not persisting, instead of silent data loss.
acceptanceCriteria: setLocalStorage emits log.warn with the error message when localStorage.setItem throws
setSessionStorage emits log.warn with the error message when sessionStorage.setItem throws
removeLocalStorage emits log.warn when localStorage.removeItem throws
removeSessionStorage emits log.warn when sessionStorage.removeItem throws
The functions still do not throw (catch-and-log, not catch-and-rethrow)
The log import is from './log.js' (relative import within the SDK)
Unit tests in storage.test.ts verify that log.warn is called when storage throws SecurityError and QuotaExceededError
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
